# Istanbul city guide

## Overview

Istanbul is the only city in the world that straddles two continents, and it feels like it. Byzantine mosaics, Ottoman mosques, rooftop terraces overlooking the Bosphorus, and some of the best street food on earth. The city hums with 2,500 years of history and a modern creative energy that's impossible to resist.

**Best time to visit**: Spring (Apr-May) and Fall (Sep-Oct) for mild weather. Summer is hot and crowded. Winter is cold but atmospheric.
**Currency**: Turkish Lira (TRY). Exchange rates fluctuate, check before you go.
**Language tip**: Turkish is the language. "Merhaba" (hello) and "tesekkur ederim" (thank you) open every door.

## Neighborhoods

| Neighborhood | Vibe | Best for | Transit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Sultanahmet | Historic, touristy | Hagia Sophia, Blue Mosque, Grand Bazaar | Tram T1, Sultanahmet Station |
| Beyoglu/Istiklal | Lively, cosmopolitan | Nightlife, cafes, art galleries, Istiklal Avenue | Metro M2, Taksim Station |
| Kadikoy (Asian side) | Trendy, local | Markets, brunch, craft beer, authentic vibe | Ferry from Eminonu or Kabatas |
| Balat | Colorful, bohemian | Photogenic streets, antique shops, cafes | Bus from Eminonu |
| Besiktas | Football, local | Fish market, waterfront, Dolmabahce Palace | Ferry or bus from Eminonu |
| Karakoy | Hip, waterfront | Street food, coffee, galleries, boutique hotels | Tram T1, Karakoy Station |
| Ortakoy | Scenic, waterfront | Bosphorus views, kumpir (baked potatoes), mosque | Bus from Besiktas |
| Nisantasi | Upscale, fashion | Luxury shopping, fine dining, stylish cafes | Metro M2, Osmanbey Station |

## Food

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description | Must-order |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Karadeniz Doner | Doner kebab | Beyoglu | $ | Legendary doner spot on Istiklal Avenue. Sliced from a massive vertical spit, served in bread or on a plate. | Doner sandwich, ayran |
| Ciya Sofrasi | Turkish/regional | Kadikoy | $$ | Chef Musa Dagdeviren serves dishes from every corner of Turkey. Anthony Bourdain called it a must-visit. | Daily changing menu, kebabs, mezes |
| Hafiz Mustafa 1864 | Dessert/cafe | Sultanahmet/Multiple | $$ | Historic Turkish sweet shop. Baklava dripping with syrup, Turkish delight in every flavor imaginable. | Pistachio baklava, Turkish delight, Turkish tea |
| Karakoy Gulluoglu | Baklava | Karakoy | $ | The baklava empire. Layers of thin pastry, pistachios, and butter. Buy a box to bring home. | Pistachio baklava, havuc dilimi |
| Pandeli | Turkish | Eminonu | $$ | A blue-tiled restaurant inside the Spice Bazaar since 1901. Classic Ottoman-era dishes with atmosphere. | Lamb stew, aubergine kebab |
| Ali Ocakbasi | Kebab | Beyoglu | $$ | Charcoal-grilled kebabs served with smoky eggplant and fresh bread. A proper Turkish grill house. | Adana kebab, patlican kebab, pide |
| Kadikoy Fish Market | Seafood | Kadikoy | $-$$ | Buy fresh fish and have it cooked at a neighboring restaurant. Balik ekmek (fish sandwich) from the stalls. | Balik ekmek (fish sandwich), grilled sea bass |
| Mikla | Fine dining | Beyoglu | $$$ | Rooftop restaurant with Bosphorus panorama. Modern Turkish-Scandinavian fusion by chef Mehmet Gurs. | Tasting menu with view |
| Kaymakci Pando | Breakfast | Besiktas | $ | Classic Turkish breakfast with kaymak (clotted cream) and honey on fresh bread. Simple and perfect. | Kaymak with honey, menemen |
| Ortakoy Kumpir Stalls | Street food | Ortakoy | $ | Giant baked potatoes stuffed with 20+ toppings. Pick your fillings, sit by the Bosphorus. | Loaded kumpir, fresh pomegranate juice |

## Landmarks and activities

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Time needed | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Hagia Sophia (Ayasofya) | Culture | Sultanahmet | Free | 1-2 hours | A 1,500-year-old architectural marvel that's been a church, a mosque, a museum, and a mosque again. The scale is staggering. |
| Blue Mosque (Sultan Ahmed Mosque) | Culture | Sultanahmet | Free | 1 hour | Six minarets, 20,000 blue Iznik tiles. Free to enter, dress modestly, visit between prayer times. |
| Grand Bazaar (Kapali Carsi) | Shopping | Sultanahmet | Free | 2-3 hours | One of the oldest covered markets in the world. Over 4,000 shops. Get lost, haggle, drink tea. |
| Topkapi Palace | History | Sultanahmet | $$ | 2-3 hours | Ottoman sultans' residence for 400 years. The Harem is extra but worth it. Treasury has the Spoonmaker's Diamond. |
| Bosphorus Cruise | Scenic | Eminonu | $-$$ | 2-3 hours | Public ferry from Eminonu to Anadolu Kavagi. Passes palaces, fortresses, and the bridges linking Europe to Asia. |
| Basilica Cistern (Yerebatan Sarnici) | History | Sultanahmet | $$ | 1 hour | Underground Byzantine water cistern with 336 columns and atmospheric lighting. Look for the Medusa heads. |
| Galata Tower | Views | Karakoy | $$ | 1 hour | Medieval stone tower with 360-degree views of the old city, Bosphorus, and Golden Horn. |
| Spice Bazaar (Misir Carsisi) | Shopping | Eminonu | Free | 1 hour | Colorful spice stalls, Turkish delight, dried fruits, and teas. More manageable than the Grand Bazaar. |
| Dolmabahce Palace | History | Besiktas | $$ | 2 hours | Opulent European-style palace on the Bosphorus. The crystal staircase and 4.5-ton chandelier are jaw-dropping. |
| Asian Side Ferry Crossing | Experience | Eminonu to Kadikoy | $ | 30 min | Take the public ferry to Kadikoy. The cheapest and most scenic cruise in Istanbul. |

## Nightlife

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Istiklal Avenue Bars | Bars | Beyoglu | $-$$ | The entire street and its side alleys are lined with bars, meyhanes (taverns), and live music venues. |
| Kadikoy Barlar Sokagi | Bars | Kadikoy | $ | "Bar street" on the Asian side. More local, cheaper, and less touristy than Beyoglu. Live music everywhere. |
| Anjelique | Rooftop club | Ortakoy | $$$ | Bosphorus-view club and restaurant. Glamorous crowd, waterfront terrace, DJ sets. |
| 360 Istanbul | Rooftop bar | Beyoglu | $$-$$$ | Panoramic views from atop a historic building near Istiklal. Cocktails with a view of the old city. |
| Meyhane (traditional tavern) | Tavern | Beyoglu/Balik Pazari | $$ | Meze platters, raki, live fasil music. Nevizade Street is lined with authentic meyhanes. |

## Transit tips

- **Istanbulkart**: Rechargeable transit card. Works on trams, metro, buses, ferries, and funiculars. Essential.
- **Tram T1**: Connects Sultanahmet, Eminonu, Karakoy, and Kabatas. The tourist backbone line.
- **Ferries**: The most scenic transit in the city. Cross to the Asian side from Eminonu or Kabatas.
- **Metro**: Expanding rapidly. M2 line connects Taksim to the airport line.
- **Dolmus**: Shared minibuses on fixed routes. Cheap and frequent. Say your stop to the driver.
- **Walking**: The old city (Sultanahmet) is very walkable. Beyoglu to Karakoy is connected by a steep hill or the Tunel funicular.
- **Pro tip**: Never take a taxi without the meter running. Use BiTaksi app for reliable rides.
- **New Airport**: Istanbul Airport (IST) is far from the city center. Allow 1-1.5 hours by Havaist bus or taxi.

## Useful Turkish phrases

| English | Turkish | Romanization |
|---|---|---|
| Hello | Merhaba | mer-HA-ba |
| Thank you | Tesekkur ederim | teh-shek-KOOR eh-deh-REEM |
| Yes/No | Evet/Hayir | eh-VET / ha-YUHR |
| How much? | Ne kadar? | neh kah-DAR |
| Delicious! | Cok guzel! | chok goo-ZEL |
| Tea please | Cay lutfen | chai LOOT-fen |
| Where is...? | ...nerede? | neh-reh-DEH |
| Cheers! | Serefe! | sheh-reh-FEH |
